At the exact 6e-4 endpoint the combination loses five of six class PQ values, DPath mPQ+ by 0.02438, 614 correctly typed nuclei, and calibration while increasing >+10 overcount prevalence by 2.11 percentage points. E44 is rejected for both objectives and must not enter E46 composition.

implementation_validation.pipeline_tests: full pipeline suite passed, including queue-order, loss-zero-equivalence, and deployment-safety checks

experiments.planned_gate: E42 is complete and rejected; E37 remains the positive exposure control. Keep label smoothing out of the primary combination so weighting-focal synergy is identifiable; admit smoothing only as a validation-gated marginal add-on. Only test sampling plus focal composition if both isolated interventions improve rare-class DQ under their independently selected learning rates.
experiments.guardrail: Reject mPQ-specific gains driven by binary-geometry loss, source-specific collapse, lower SQ, or an unexpanded LR boundary. Report directional count tails, but do not use the count metric to veto a separate segmentation model selected specifically for mPQ+.

experiments.name: hovernet_class_sampling_intensity_refinement
experiments.status: implementation validated; waiter serialized after E43 diagnostic backfill
experiments.change: Reduce E37's fixed class-aware replacement-sampling mass from 0.5 to 0.25 or 0.10 while preserving the seed, split, architecture, optimizer-step budget, and official augmentation/loss recipe.
experiments.selection_metric: development-validation mPQ+ and R2 selected independently; each fraction receives its own full LR bracket and two-epoch metric cadence

causal_trigger.interpretation: The 0.5 intervention passes the mPQ/DQ/SQ gate but fails R2 and worsens count tails, so intensity—not composition—is the next isolated variable.

planned_grid.practical_control: exact seed-206 ordinary uniform E37 grid without replacement
planned_grid.mechanism_control: new seed-206 uniform-with-replacement grid at every learning rate

experiments.queue_order: Wait for the E43-specific validation-backfill marker, then run all six candidates serially. This preserves the existing E41/E42/E44→E43 chain and prevents GPU overlap.
experiments.planned_next_gate: A lower fraction is an mPQ candidate only if its validation-selected mPQ/mDQ each beat both independently selected ordinary uniform and uniform-with-replacement controls by >0.003, both mSQ losses are under 0.005, CRAG/DPath/GLaS mPQ/mDQ/mSQ stay above -0.01 versus both controls, and its learning-rate/horizon selection is confirmed off a boundary. This separates class-exposure benefit from replacement/duplication regularization without hiding source collapse. Count tails do not veto that metric-specific route. A separate early-heavy tail-repair/Pareto screen additionally requires improved absolute signed bias versus class-0.5, improvement in at least one >10/>20 outlier proportion, and no material MAE/outlier regression. Replacing class-0.5 for mPQ also requires mPQ and mDQ within 0.005 of its frontier.

sampler_prior_correction.scope: Use realized per-epoch development-training nucleus exposure to correct the artificial replacement-sampling prior back toward the natural development-training nucleus prior. This is not supervised estimation of locked-test frequencies.
sampler_prior_correction.validation: Select correction strength excluding each institution, route each held institution through its excluded-source choice, and require pooled out-of-source mPQ/mDQ improvement, mSQ safety, stable held-source strengths, and no mPQ/mDQ/mSQ regression below -0.01 in CRAG, DPath, or GLaS. Count tails remain a separate R2/reliability diagnostic and do not veto mPQ correction.
sampler_prior_correction.integration: Correction is now part of the E45 promotion report rather than an unconsumed side artifact.

experiments.guardrail: WeightedRandomSampler changes both class exposure and replacement behavior: on the exact development-training fold, class fractions 0.10/0.25/0.50 have expected unique-patch fractions 0.630/0.622/0.597 per epoch, while ordinary sampling sees every patch. Require the uniform-with-replacement mechanism control before attributing a gain to class weighting. Do not select a fraction at the 0.5 model's LR without checking its own LR response. Report under- and over-count tails separately, distinguish a safer Pareto candidate from the outright mPQ winner, and keep this endpoint out of the R2 recipe unless its independently selected count score and tails pass. Do not combine with rejected E44 weighting/focal loss.

experiments.name: metric_specific_validation_gated_composition
experiments.status: raw-map inference and metric-specific gates implemented; robust count-correction pilot is a held Pareto candidate, membership still awaits E41-E45 artifacts
experiments.change: Build separate mPQ+ and R2 recipes from validation-qualified endpoints instead of forcing one model or fusion rule to optimize both leaderboard metrics.
experiments.evaluation_set: 711-patch source-group-disjoint development validation for membership and weight selection; locked test remains untouched until recipes are frozen

pilot_result.scope: robust R2 count-correction subcandidate only; no locked-test inference

experiments.selection_metric: mPQ+ branch: development-validation pooled mPQ+ over a bounded pre-decoder map-weight grid, with DQ/SQ guardrails. R2 branch: classwise development-validation R2 over a bounded count-weight grid, with leave-one-source-out residual and tail checks.

mpq_recipe.candidate_gate: A member must be competitive alone, improve pooled mPQ+ and mDQ+ by more than 0.003 without an mSQ+ loss beyond 0.005, retain a stable blend weight when each source is excluded, avoid mPQ+/mDQ+/mSQ+ regressions below -0.01 in CRAG, DPath, or GLaS, and exhibit nonredundant per-class/source TP-FP-FN or confusion errors. Count tails are reported but cannot veto this fixed-geometry mPQ route.
mpq_recipe.fusion: First screen shared NP/HV/TP checkpoint weights. If a candidate improves typing but fails the geometry/SQ guardrail, preserve the selected geometry model's decoded instances and validation-sweep instance-matched type probabilities; branch-specific pre-decoder TP weights remain available if that cheaper fixed-geometry screen passes.
mpq_recipe.selection: Maximize development-validation mPQ+; use mDQ+, mSQ+, rare-class false-positive tails, and worst-source behavior as guardrails rather than secondary tuning targets.

r2_recipe.candidate_gate: Report standalone candidate improvement separately from composition. A blend qualifies only if leave-one-source-out class weights beat the better endpoint by >0.003 R2, the full-validation gain is also >0.003, the full-versus-held-source gap is at most 0.02, MAE, absolute signed bias, and >10/>20 outlier prevalence stay within predeclared tolerances, and no supported source×class true-zero stratum increases its >10 or >20 false-count prevalence beyond a support-aware tolerance of max(base rate, two patches / stratum support).
r2_recipe.fusion: Validation-select bounded count-vector weights independently by class, while retaining the separately selected mPQ+ masks and types.
r2_recipe.stability_check: Compare full-validation weights with leave-one-source-out selections and report signed bias, MAE, and absolute-error outlier fractions at 5, 10, and 20 cells. Separately report true-zero support and false-count prevalence overall, by class, by source, and by source×class; supported strata gate admission at >10 and >20 cells.

robust_count_correction_pilot.evaluation_set: 711-patch source-group-disjoint development validation; no test inference
robust_count_correction_pilot.method: Retain the E33 classwise convex count weights, but cap auxiliary additions and removals separately in multiples of sqrt(mask_count+1). Positive caps prevent the auxiliary stream from inventing many cells without mask support; negative caps can remain looser where the auxiliary stream reliably removes mask overcounts.
robust_count_correction_pilot.selection: For each class, select the ordinary E32/E28 convex weight first, then select positive and negative cap scales on validation R2 subject to the supported source×class true-zero >10/>20 gate. Repeat both stages inside five source-stratified GroupKFold splits and in leave-one-institution-out analysis.
robust_count_correction_pilot.decision: Retain as a reliability-oriented Pareto candidate, but do not replace E33 or run locked-test inference yet. Its full-validation gain is below the predeclared +0.003 practical threshold, the nested bootstrap CI crosses zero, three of five CV folds regress, and OOF general-error tails rise slightly despite much safer true-zero behavior.

experiments.guardrail: Do not blend every apparent winner. Reject candidates with redundant residuals, source-local gains, unstable class weights, upper-bound selections that have not been expanded, or improvements visible only on the retrospective internal test. Run the no-TTA pilot composition first; add six-view TTA only after membership and weights are frozen.
